rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.title: Three Sisters (agriculture) — Wikipedia
results.reason: Source-domain Wikipedia article on the agricultural concept itself; no LLM/MAS content.

results.title: Mixture-of-Agents (MoA) Enhances LLM (2024/6)
results.reason: MoA uses layered specialized agents but does not enforce a 3-role asymmetric non-circular DAG with distinct resource types per role.

results.title: Adaptive Task Graphs for LLM Agent Teams (2026/5)
results.reason: Adaptive task DAGs over LLM agents match TSARM's DAG cooperation topology (sem and func both above threshold).

results.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration Mechanisms — Survey of LLMs (2025/1)
results.reason: Survey explicitly catalogs 3-agent role-specialized teams (two experts + summarizer), directly the 3-role asymmetric structure of TSARM.

results.title: Multi-Agent Teams Hold Experts Back (2026/2)
results.reason: Empirical study of team underperformance; doesn't propose a 3-role asymmetric DAG mechanism.

results.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration via Evolving Orchestration (2025/5)
results.reason: Relies on a central evolving orchestrator, opposite of TSARM's no-orchestrator topology.

results.title: Latent Collaboration in Multi-Agent Systems (2025/11)
results.reason: Latent shared-memory MAS achieves the same mutual-support primitive (sem and func both at threshold).

results.title: AgentSymbiotic — Web Agents (2025/2)
results.reason: 2-agent symbiotic distillation; different cardinality and mechanism from 3-role asymmetric DAG.

fail_reason: Three results trigger hits via sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7: rank 3 (Adaptive Task Graphs, sem 0.72/func 0.73) matches the DAG-cooperation primitive; rank 4 (MAS Survey, sem 0.78/func 0.85) explicitly documents 3-agent role-specialized teams; rank 7 (Latent Collaboration, sem 0.70/func 0.72) achieves the equivalent mutual-support MAS primitive. TSARM's 3-role asymmetric non-circular DAG is not sufficiently novel against existing role-specialized and DAG-based multi-agent literature.
